title: sw-dev-blueprint EM diagnosis (tasks/diagnosis.json)
description: Schema-bound verdict the EM writes when the shell orchestrator consults it after a task fails twice, or when the full frozen suite fails after all tasks passed (spec drift). The shell routes MECHANICALLY on `verdict` — the EM advises, it never drives (D-26/D-29). The orchestrator validates this file with scripts/validate-plan.py --diagnosis, consumes it, and deletes it; it is never committed.

task_id.description: The failing task id (e.g. T3), or the literal string DRIFT for a suite-red/tasks-green consult.

verdict.description: brief_wrong: the task brief under-specified or mis-specified the work — supply revised_brief. decomposition_wrong: the task split itself is wrong (wrong file boundaries, missing task, bad dependency) — the orchestrator will ask for a plan re-emit. contract_or_test_wrong: the frozen spec (contracts.json entry or TPM test) is wrong — this escalates to the TPM as a batched bundle; `reason` becomes the evidence the human operator carries to the web chat.

reason.description: Plain-language explanation of the verdict, written to be read by the tier above (orchestrator log, or the TPM via the escalation bundle).

revised_brief.description: REQUIRED when verdict is brief_wrong: the full replacement brief (Rule 8 discipline applies). Ignored otherwise.
